reasoning_steps: 2. Traced upstream path: WAN-MPLS-CORE → BR-RTR-01 → BR-FW-01 → BR-SW-01 → BR-APP-01.
3. WAN-MPLS-CORE has highest topological reach — zero or minimal in-degree, maximum out-degree.
4. All impacted nodes (BR-RTR-01, BR-FW-01, BR-SW-01…) are reachable from WAN-MPLS-CORE.
recommended_actions: Check WAN / MPLS handoff status and verify circuit health
Validate router uplink interface metrics (packet loss, latency, CRC errors)
Review firewall tunnel and NAT policy logs for recent changes
Notify network operations if upstream packet loss continues
symptoms: Endpoint reports application access failures and packet loss
Access switch sees downstream impact from multiple workstations
Firewall / router is on the access path for all affected flows
WAN handoff is the common upstream dependency
why_root: WAN-MPLS-CORE is the common upstream dependency for the impacted branch topology path. The affected node BR-APP-01 depends on: WAN-MPLS-CORE → BR-RTR-01 → BR-FW-01 → BR-SW-01 → BR-APP-01.
